avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in some cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the large surface-area wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered now as before.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude. On steep sunny slopes the snowpack will only just freeze.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes more dry slab avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

Meticulous route selection is required. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1200 m. As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate wind from westerly directions, further wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers on wind-protected shady slopes.
Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of rounded crystals and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is not capable of bearing a load, in particular on steep sunny slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a considerable danger of dry avalanches will be encountered over a wide area. This applies in particular above the tree line.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, further wind slabs formed. The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in all aspects and generally above the tree line. This applies in particular on steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanches can be released in the new snow and wind slab layers and reach large size.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.
Below approximately 1500 m small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 1200 m. As a consequence of snowfall and the moderate westerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers.
Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of rounded crystals and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is not capable of bearing a load, in particular on sunny slopes.
The snowpack will become moist at low altitude.

Above approximately 1500 m a lot of snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
Considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
The wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground. They can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant.  Wooded areas could also be affected by avalanches and present situations of instability. Moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.
The wind slabs of the last few days are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Over a wide area light snowfall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Avalanches can as before be released, even by a single winter sport participant. The snowpack remains prone to triggering.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. They can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in isolated cases. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 1800 m as well as in areas close to the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 1800 m as well as in areas close to the tree line.

Steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m: The snowpack will be moist. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times.

tendency.highlights: A treacherous avalanche situation will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In particular along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is slightly greater.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches. They can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent in the afternoon. The soft wind slabs will become increasingly prone to triggering in particular on north to east to south facing aspects.
As the snowfall becomes more intense individual natural avalanches are possible as the day progresses, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Tuesday: 20 to 40 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1600 m.
The fresh snow and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow and partly also surface hoar. In some cases the wind slabs have bonded poorly with the old snowpack.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Since Friday on very steep slopes several medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches occurred naturally. Some small and medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last three days.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will increase quickly during the day. The large quantity of fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Avalanches can as before be released, even by a single winter sport participant. The snowpack remains prone to triggering.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. Mostly they are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m,, in isolated cases also in areas close to the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 2000 m. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m,, in isolated cases also in areas close to the tree line.

Steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m: The snowpack will be moist. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. A sometimes treacherous avalanche situation will persist.

bulletins.highlights: The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. The avalanche danger above the tree line is within the uppermost range of danger level 3 (considerable).
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes. The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced over a wide area. The snowpack will be moist at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will persist. Above the tree line a treacherous avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in steep terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs represent the main danger. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas and on steep, little used slopes. At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and larger.
The avalanches are medium-sized and can mostly only be released by large loads.
In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on wind-loaded slopes. The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on shady slopes.
Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of rounded crystals and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is not capable of bearing a load, especially on steep shady slopes.
Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on shady slopes, especially at intermediate and high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible below approximately 1900 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are treacherous.

snowpackStructure.comment: In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls. In some places new snow is lying on a hard crust.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As you climb higher in altitude, danger spots are more widespread.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches. They can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent in the afternoon. The soft wind slabs will become increasingly prone to triggering in particular on north to east to south facing aspects.
As the snowfall becomes more intense individual natural avalanches are possible in the evening, but they will be mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Tuesday: 5 to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1600 m.
The fresh snow and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow and partly also surface hoar.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Since Friday on very steep slopes several small to medium-sized avalanches occurred naturally. Isolated small to medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last three days.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will increase quickly during the day.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs of last week represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Over a wide area easily released wind slabs formed. The wind slabs are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar above the tree line. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially on steep shady slopes, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. In some cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

Low and intermediate altitudes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. Gliding avalanches can also occur.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
Gliding avalanches can also occur at any time.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
